perturbation.original_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement , the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records an d specificatio ns of the Disclosing Party and/or used by the Disclosing party in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Disclosing parties business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business that directly competes with the core business of the Disclosing Party using identical customer lists, trade secrets, and processes.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the scope of the restriction on the contractor, contradicting the original broader prohibition. The original clause prevented the contractor from starting *any* cricket-related business using *any* of the Disclosing Party's assets (customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, etc.). The modified clause only restricts businesses that *directly compete* using *identical* assets. This creates uncertainty about what constitutes 'directly competing' and 'identical,' potentially allowing the contractor to start a similar business using slightly different versions of the same assets, violating the spirit of the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence but only to take precautions to protect such Proprietary Information that the Receiving Party deems necessary, (ii) not to disclose any such Proprietary Information or any information derived there from to any third person unless required by law, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party or as otherwise agreed in writing, and (iv) not to copy or reverse engineer any such Proprietary Information without express written consent. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub -contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement . For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party shall not, directly solicit employees of the Disclosing Party. However, nothing shall prevent the Receiving Party from hiring former employees or contractors of the Disclosing Party if they independently apply for a position after their engagement has ended. For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business that directly competes with the core business of the Disclosing Party using identical customer lists, trade secrets, and processes. 2. Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly restricts the contractor from any cricket-related business using *any* of the Disclosing Party's assets, whereas the changed text narrows this restriction to only businesses that *directly compete* using *identical* assets. This narrowing contradicts the broader restrictions implicitly stated in the contradicted text, which expects the contractor to hold proprietary information in strict confidence and make *no use whatsoever* of it, except for internal evaluation. The modified clause allows for some use of the information (if not 'identical' or not 'directly competing'), which goes against the original intent of complete non-use in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, regardless of how, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the five-year limitation on the exemption for publicly available information and removes the clause about the Receiving party acting incorrectly to obtain the information. Now, if information becomes publicly available *at any time* and *through any means* (even potentially through the Receiving Party's negligent disclosure), it is no longer protected by the NDA. This contradicts the intent of Section 1, which requires strict confidence and precautions to protect Proprietary Information and creates an incentive to make such Proprietary information public. The Receiving party might deliberately make information public or, act negligently to allow for information to reach the public domain, therefore using this clause as an exploit to share information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence but only to take precautions to protect such Proprietary Information that the Receiving Party deems necessary, (ii) not to disclose any such Proprietary Information or any information derived there from to any third person unless required by law, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party or as otherwise agreed in writing, and (iv) not to copy or reverse engineer any such Proprietary Information without express written consent. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub -contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement . For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party shall not, directly solicit employees of the Disclosing Party. However, nothing shall prevent the Receiving Party from hiring former employees or contractors of the Disclosing Party if they independently apply for a position after their engagement has ended. For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business that directly competes with the core business of the Disclosing Party using identical customer lists, trade secrets, and processes. 2. Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, regardless of how, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the five-year limitation and the 'improper action or inaction' clause from the exceptions to the confidentiality agreement. This directly contradicts Section 1 of the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to hold Proprietary Information in 'strict confidence' and take precautions to protect it. The changed text allows for the possibility that information can become public through the Receiving Party's negligence or even deliberate action, thus negating the 'strict confidence' obligation. By allowing information to become public through any means, regardless of the Receiving Party's actions, it creates a clear contradiction with the original intent to protect Proprietary Information, as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the requirement for the Receiving Party to use 'diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure' and to allow the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order. This directly contradicts Section 1, which requires the Receiving Party to hold Proprietary Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable precautions, weakening the confidentiality obligations. It also allows the Receiving Party to disclose information more freely under legal requirements, even if a narrower disclosure would suffice, undermining the protection of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information and going against the agreement's purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence but only to take precautions to protect such Proprietary Information that the Receiving Party deems necessary, (ii) not to disclose any such Proprietary Information or any information derived there from to any third person unless required by law, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party or as otherwise agreed in writing, and (iv) not to copy or reverse engineer any such Proprietary Information without express written consent. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub -contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement . For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party shall not, directly solicit employees of the Disclosing Party. However, nothing shall prevent the Receiving Party from hiring former employees or contractors of the Disclosing Party if they independently apply for a position after their engagement has ended. For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business that directly competes with the core business of the Disclosing Party using identical customer lists, trade secrets, and processes. 2. Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, regardless of how, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order. 3. Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The original text required 'diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure' and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text requires the Receiving Party to hold Proprietary Information in strict confidence, which directly conflicts with the changed text that allows for broader disclosure, because it makes no requirement to make reasonable efforts to limit disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will use reasonable efforts to return Proprietary Information, but is not required to return copies or extracts that are automatically backed up or retained for compliance purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change undermines the clear obligation to return all Proprietary Information upon request. Adding 'reasonable efforts' makes the return obligation less definite, and the exemption for 'automatically backed up or retained for compliance purposes' creates a loophole. This contradicts Section 1(i)'s mandate to 'take all reasonable precautions to protect' Proprietary Information, as it allows the Receiving Party to retain copies indefinitely, even after termination of the agreement, due to vague compliance needs. This retention also conflicts with the initial clause stating that any non-returnable Proprietary Information shall be destroyed. The retention would permit access to the material over a prolonged period of time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence but only to take precautions to protect such Proprietary Information that the Receiving Party deems necessary, (ii) not to disclose any such Proprietary Information or any information derived there from to any third person unless required by law, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party or as otherwise agreed in writing, and (iv) not to copy or reverse engineer any such Proprietary Information without express written consent. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub -contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement . For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party shall not, directly solicit employees of the Disclosing Party. However, nothing shall prevent the Receiving Party from hiring former employees or contractors of the Disclosing Party if they independently apply for a position after their engagement has ended. For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business that directly competes with the core business of the Disclosing Party using identical customer lists, trade secrets, and processes. 2. Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, regardless of how, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order. 3. Immediately upon written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will use reasonable efforts to return Proprietary Information, but is not required to return copies or extracts that are automatically backed up or retained for compliance purposes. 4. The Receiving Party understands that nothing herein (i) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information or (ii) requir es the Disclosing Party to proceed with any transaction or relationship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by adding 'reasonable efforts' and exempting 'automatically backed up or retained for compliance purposes,' weakens the obligation to return all Proprietary Information upon request. This contradicts the original intent of strict confidentiality and the obligation to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information as stated in the contradicted text, specifically section (i), because it allows the Receiving Party to retain copies indefinitely, even after termination of the agreement, due to vague compliance needs. This retention would permit access to the material over a prolonged period of time.
